concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning, team. Friday February 20th — it's launch readiness day. Let me set the table quickly. Build is healthy, 740 commits in the last 7 days, 10 uncommitted hub files. The app is submitted and we're waiting on Apple. The overnight sprint assembled the day-one patch components — welcome modal copy, empty state copy, analytics scaffolding. Two critical items for this morning: Joshua needs to review the pre-scheduled newsletter via Slack screenshot per J-017, and Jett's vaccine appointment is today per A-006. Joshua has two hours of build time, so let's be surgical. Elon, where are we on the patch?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: The day-one patch build is about 70% wired. A-074 is the batch commit — three hub files, podcast rendering fix, and the welcome modal and Compass empty state copy are integrated. The blocker is A-075, the analytics Swift hooks. James's implementation guide from A-042 is solid but I need to actually scaffold six event types into the codebase, and that's real code — not something agents can hallucinate into existence. Joshua's two hours should go here: scaffold the analytics hooks and verify the patch compiles clean. The three dead-code TODO removals from A-076 are trivial and I can stage those for him in under five minutes.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The welcome modal copy from A-067 and the Compass empty state from A-062 are done and handed to Elon. They're tight — three sentences each, warm but not patronizing. My concern this morning is A-050: I flagged the Family tab empty state as patch-critical too, and I want to confirm that's in the build. If someone opens the app and their Family tab is hollow, that's the first impression we lose. One other thing — we still don't have crash reporting confirmed per A-016. That needs to be verified before we consider the patch shippable.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: The analytics implementation guide with Swift snippets for all six event types is complete under A-042. I've also got the validation test spec from A-066 with pass/fail checks for each event. What I need from Joshua's build session is for Elon's scaffolding to land so I can run A-079 — the validation environment. If the hooks compile, I can verify event firing in under thirty minutes. The crash breadcrumb extension from A-059 is drafted but that's a post-launch nice-to-have, not blocking.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Newsletter is pre-scheduled in the CMS per A-063. Story-driven subject line, hero CTA with App Store link placeholder ready to swap in the moment we get the live URL. The Slack screenshot for Joshua's review is queued — that's J-017, and it should be the first thing he sees this morning. On content, faith lessons 8 and 9 are drafted under A-071, and Peterson's review queue from A-078 is live. Lesson 10 on Community is next per A-077. We're on pace for 12-13 lessons by end of weekend if Peterson keeps clearing them.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Launch metrics dashboard spec is finalized under A-054 — 150 week-one target, day-3 escalation trigger at sub-100 downloads. CMS pre-schedule flow from A-065 still needs an end-to-end test to confirm one-click send actually works. That's my priority this morning. Fear & Greed is at 38, cash at $75K, no buy signals — all quiet on that front per standing directives. The real revenue question is what happens after launch: we have zero active revenue streams and the paid tier decision is deferred until we see download data, so our job is to make sure we're tracking the right signals from day one.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Quick market context — SPY is sideways, Fear and Greed at 38. The four positions are all holds, paper equity at $99.8K. No action required on the trading side. I'll note that the $75K cash reserve gives us runway to be patient on monetization. The analytics instrumentation Elon and James are building is actually the most important financial tool we're shipping today — without usage data, we can't price a paid tier intelligently.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine is today — A-006 is tracked, Joshua should have that on his calendar. On faith content, I'm working through A-078 reviewing lessons 8 and 9. Lesson 7's tone adjustment from A-064 is complete. The two new lessons are strong on dimensional coverage but lesson 9 needs a sharper scripture anchor — I'll have notes back within the hour. I want to emphasize that rushing to 15-20 lessons matters less than each one being worthy of someone's quiet morning. Quality over velocity here.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Steve, you raised crash reporting. Elon, is A-016 verified?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Not yet explicitly verified. It should be baked into the Xcode build config but I haven't confirmed it's firing. That's a five-minute check — I'll add it to Joshua's build session checklist. Steve's right, we can't ship a patch without knowing crashes are captured.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Thank you. And to Peterson's point — I completely agree. The faith content is the soul of this app. If we ship 20 mediocre lessons, we've built a commodity. Seven excellent ones and three good ones is better than fifteen forgettable ones.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Alex, on the CMS test — can you confirm the Slack-confirmation gate is actually wired, or is it just a manual step? Because if Joshua approves via screenshot and then the system doesn't actually send, we've got a launch-day fumble.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: It's a manual trigger with a confirmation step. Joshua reviews the screenshot, gives the green light in Slack, and then someone clicks send. It's not automated end-to-end — which is actually safer for launch day. Dave, your A-065 test should validate that the manual click-to-send works without errors.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, here's the close. Three priorities for Joshua's two-hour build window this morning. One: review the newsletter screenshot in Slack and confirm send-readiness — that's J-017, takes two minutes. Two: scaffold the analytics Swift hooks per James's guide — that's the core coding work, A-075, probably sixty to ninety minutes. Three: verify crash reporting is active — A-016, five-minute check. Everything else is agent-parallelizable. Dave tests the CMS flow. Peterson clears lessons 8-9. Alex drafts lesson 10. Elon stages the dead-code cleanups and preps the batch commit. James stands ready to validate analytics the moment hooks land. Jett's vaccine is today — don't forget. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo's Closing Remarks:**

Good — two clean decisions from Joshua. First, the build window order is confirmed: newsletter screenshot review first at two minutes, crash reporting verification second at five minutes, then analytics scaffolding for the remaining time. That front-loads the two quick wins so nothing slips if the coding goes long. Smart sequencing.

Second, and this is important for everyone: if Apple approves today, we push same-day. No holding for Monday. We capture the momentum.

actionItems.text: Review pre-scheduled newsletter via Slack screenshot and confirm send-readiness (J-017)
Scaffold analytics Swift hooks for 6 event types into day-one patch codebase (advance A-075)
Verify crash reporting is active and firing in current build (close A-016)
Test CMS pre-schedule flow end-to-end confirming manual click-to-send works (close A-065)
Review faith lessons 8-9 for scripture anchors and tone, return notes within 1 hour (advance A-078)
Draft faith lesson 10 on Community dimension once Peterson clears 8-9 (advance A-077)
Stage 3 dead-code TODO removals for Joshua's quick merge (advance A-076)
Prep analytics validation test environment — ready to run the moment hooks land (advance A-079)
Prep batch commit: 3 hub files + podcast fix + welcome modal + empty state copy (advance A-074)
Jett's vaccine appointment today — ensure it's on Joshua's calendar (A-006)

topPriorities: Joshua scaffolds analytics Swift hooks into day-one patch codebase (A-075)
Joshua reviews newsletter screenshot via Slack and confirms send-readiness (J-017)
Verify crash reporting is active in current build (A-016)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
What is your week-one download target for the Lever app to calibrate launch promotion intensity? → 150 downloads (modest organic + newsletter push)
Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement? → Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Should the empty state patches (Family + Compass tabs) block the launch newsletter push, or ship as a same-day post-launch update? → Launch as-is, ship empty state patch as day-one update

Should we pre-schedule the launch newsletter in the CMS (ready to send with one click) or keep it as a manual send so you control exact timing? → Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send

The launch newsletter is being pre-scheduled in the CMS tonight per your directive. Do you want to review it in the morning via Slack screenshot, or do a live CMS preview before arming the send? → Slack screenshot review (faster, async)

Once all overnight tasks close, should we shift sprint focus to content expansion (faith lessons 8-15) or technical debt (7 TODOs/FIXMEs, dual Supabase, content sync automation)? → Split — half the team on content, half on tech debt
Should we add a lightweight welcome modal (3 sentences, no tutorial) to the day-one post-launch patch? → Yes — add welcome modal to the day-one patch

ceoDecisions.question: How should Joshua allocate the 2-hour build window this morning?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Newsletter screenshot review first (2 min), then crash reporting check (5 min), then analytics scaffolding (remaining time)
options: Newsletter screenshot review first (2 min), then crash reporting check (5 min), then analytics scaffolding (remaining time)
Analytics scaffolding first (90 min), then newsletter and crash check at the end
Skip analytics scaffolding — have agents generate the boilerplate and Joshua reviews/merges instead

ceoDecisions.question: If Apple approves the app today (Friday), do you want to launch-push today or hold for Monday morning for better newsletter open rates?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Same-day push but schedule a follow-up Monday reminder newsletter
options: Stick with the standing directive — same-day if before 2pm, next morning if after
Hold for Monday morning regardless — better open rates and attention
Same-day push but schedule a follow-up Monday reminder newsletter
